title: Twin studies highlight the role of genes in school success
text:
I am NOT a bondsman and in the late
1980s I established the Netherlands twin
register at the paralysis attack in
Amsterdam because we run a large stream
register we also get get lots of
questions from parents and from society
so questions from parents when my
children go to school should they be
going to the same classroom should be
should I place them in different schools
what is better for one child was dead
before the other child and astonishingly
there were no answers to these questions
so we looked in our database and
basically what we found doesn't make a
difference if the genome contribute to
how children do in school for example
how good they are in arithmetic how well
they read whether they have problems
with writing and reading for all these
strengths we would expect identical
prints to be more alike if the genome
contributes to differences between
children and this is actually what we
see there have been some very large
studies in which in multiple groups of
people DNA variants were characterized
and tested for their predictive power to
see how well people get in educational
attainment and it's growing very rapidly
at the moment there are nearly a hundred
genetic variants that have been
identified as explained part of the
heritability of educational attainment
